title.en: LLM Agents Can See Code Repositories
title.zh: LLM智能体可以看到代码仓库

abstract: Coding agents powered by large language models have demonstrated strong performance on software engineering tasks. Yet most agents consume repositories almost entirely as text, which differs from how human developers use visual structure such as folder hierarchies and dependency relationships to orient themselves in large codebases. With multimodal large language models (MLLMs), it is an open question whether agents can effectively benefit from visual representations of repositories. This paper presents the first systematic empirical study of visual repository representations for LLM-based agents on repository-level issue resolution. We evaluate four recent multimodal models. Our results show that a strictly vision-only setup degrades accuracy and increases token cost, because agents lack sufficient symbolic detail and compensate with repeated visual queries. In contrast, integrating visual graphs of repository structure as a supplementary modality alongside standard text interfaces helps agents understand structure more efficiently: input token consumption decreases by up to 26% while issue-resolution accuracy is maintained or improved. Visualization is most useful during fault localization and when the agent autonomously controls exploration depth. These findings point to a practical hybrid text-and-vision design for next-generation coding agents.
comment: ASE 2026. Our code and data are available at https://github.com/cslsolow/SeeRepo

relevance.en: This paper is tangential to your focus on post-training techniques like RLHF or execution feedback—it studies input representation for agents, not training. However, it may interest you because it shows that visual repository graphs can reduce token consumption by 26%, a practical efficiency gain relevant for real-world deployment of repository-level agents.
relevance.zh: 本文与您关注的RLHF或执行反馈等后训练技术略有不同——它研究的是智能体的输入表示，而非训练。不过，本文可能引起您的兴趣，因为它表明可视化仓库图可以将令牌消耗减少26%，这是一个与仓库级智能体实际部署相关的实际效率提升。

brief_summary.en:
Current LLM-based coding agents consume code repositories as plain text, ignoring the visual structure—folder hierarchies, dependency graphs—that human developers rely on to navigate large codebases. This forces agents to run repeated shell commands like `ls` and `grep`, wasting tokens and interaction rounds. To address this, the paper introduces SeeRepo, a framework that supplements standard text interfaces with visual representations of repository structure, such as dependency graphs and folder trees rendered as images.

SeeRepo operates within a ReAct-style agent loop where the multimodal LLM can request either text or visual inputs. Experiments on SWE-bench Verified show that this hybrid design reduces input token consumption by up to 26% while maintaining or slightly improving issue-resolution accuracy (e.g., GPT-5-mini: 38.2% text-only vs. 38.8% with SeeRepo). The visual modality is most beneficial during fault localization and when the agent controls its own exploration depth. In contrast, a purely vision-only setup degrades accuracy and increases token cost due to lack of symbolic detail. These findings point to a practical hybrid approach that balances structural overview with precise text.
brief_summary.zh:
当前的基于LLM的编码智能体将代码仓库视为纯文本来处理，忽略了视觉结构——文件夹层次结构、依赖图——而这些是人类开发者在导航大型代码库时所依赖的。这迫使智能体反复运行shell命令，如`ls`和`grep`，浪费了令牌和交互轮次。为了解决这个问题，本文引入了SeeRepo，这是一个框架，通过仓库结构的视觉表示（例如依赖图和文件夹树）作为图像来补充标准文本接口。

SeeRepo在一个ReAct风格的智能体循环中运行，其中多模态LLM可以请求文本或视觉输入。在SWE-bench Verified上的实验表明，这种混合设计减少了高达26%的输入令牌消耗，同时保持或略微提高了问题解决准确率（例如，GPT-5-mini：纯文本38.2% vs. 使用SeeRepo 38.8%）。视觉模态在故障定位和智能体自主控制探索深度时最为有益。相比之下，纯视觉设置由于缺乏符号细节而降低了准确率并增加了令牌成本。这些发现指向了一种实用的混合方法，平衡了结构概览和精确文本。

problem.en: Current LLM-based agents for software engineering tasks consume code repositories almost entirely as text, ignoring the visual structure (e.g., folder hierarchies, dependency graphs, file trees) that human developers rely on for orientation. This textual-only approach leads to inefficient exploration: agents must iteratively run shell commands (ls, grep) to locate relevant files, incurring high token consumption and many interaction rounds. Prior work on coding agents focused on text-based retrieval, editing, or reasoning, but did not systematically investigate whether multimodal large language models (MLLMs) can benefit from visual representations of repository structure. The specific gap is a lack of empirical study on using visual graphs as a supplementary modality for repository-level issue resolution, which matters because large codebases contain structural information that is costly to capture in text alone and could reduce redundant context if presented visually.
problem.zh: 当前的基于LLM的软件工程任务智能体几乎完全将代码仓库视为文本处理，忽略了视觉结构（例如文件夹层次结构、依赖图、文件树），而这些是人类开发者用于定位的依赖。这种纯文本方法导致低效的探索：智能体必须反复运行shell命令（ls、grep）来定位相关文件，导致高令牌消耗和许多交互轮次。先前的编码智能体工作集中在基于文本的检索、编辑或推理上，但没有系统研究多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）是否能从仓库结构的视觉表示中受益。具体的空白是缺乏关于将视觉图作为仓库级问题解决补充模态的实证研究，这一点很重要，因为大型代码库包含仅用文本捕捉成本高昂的结构信息，如果以视觉方式呈现可以减少冗余上下文。

solution.en: The paper introduces SeeRepo, a framework that integrates visual graphs of repository structure (e.g., dependency graphs, folder trees) as a supplementary modality alongside standard text-based code access for LLM agents. The key insight is a hybrid text-and-vision design: visual overviews provide efficient structural understanding while textual code preserves the symbolic detail necessary for precise editing. Unlike a purely vision-only setup, which degrades accuracy and increases token cost due to lack of detail (agents compensate with repeated visual queries), SeeRepo uses images only to guide exploration and reduce navigation overhead. The agent autonomously controls when to consult visual graphs versus reading text, making exploration depth a decision variable. This differs from prior work by being the first systematic empirical study of visual representations for repository-level issue resolution, demonstrating that visual aids are most beneficial during fault localization and when the agent controls exploration depth.
solution.zh: 本文介绍了SeeRepo，这是一个框架，将仓库结构的视觉图（例如依赖图、文件夹树）作为LLM智能体的补充模态，与标准基于文本的代码访问相结合。关键见解是一种混合文本与视觉设计：视觉概览提供高效的结构理解，而文本代码保留了精确编辑所需的符号细节。与纯视觉设置不同，纯视觉设置由于缺乏细节而降低准确率并增加令牌成本（智能体通过重复视觉查询补偿），SeeRepo仅使用图像来指导探索并减少导航开销。智能体自主控制何时查阅视觉图与阅读文本，使探索深度成为决策变量。这与先前工作的不同之处在于，它是第一个系统研究仓库级问题解决中视觉表示的实证研究，证明了视觉辅助在故障定位和智能体控制探索深度时最为有益。

methodology.en: SeeRepo operates within a ReAct-style agent loop designed for issue resolution on SWE-bench Verified. At each step, the agent can request: (1) text representations (source code, shell output) and (2) visual representations (structural graphs rendered as images). The visual graphs are generated offline from the repository using static analysis: a dependency graph (inter-module imports), a folder tree (hierarchical directory structure), and an AST snippet (code structure for a specific file). These are rendered as PNG images with clear labels. When the agent chooses to view an image, the MLLM processes it as follows (Figure 1): the input image I∈R^{H×W×3} is divided into fixed-size patches {p_i}, each encoded by a Vision Transformer (ViT) into visual tokens, which are projected into the LLM's embedding space via a projection layer and concatenated with text tokens. This preserves spatial topology for reasoning about dependencies. The agent's action space includes file reading, shell commands, and image viewing. Important design decisions: (1) visual graphs are supplementary—the agent still accesses text, avoiding the loss of symbolic detail seen in vision-only setups; (2) the agent autonomously determines when to request an image (exploration depth is not predetermined); (3) three visualization styles (folder tree, dependency graph, AST snippet) are compared. The framework is evaluated using four recent multimodal LLMs (including GPT-5-mini and GPT-4o). The visual graphs are generated once per repository and cached; no retraining is involved.
methodology.zh: SeeRepo在一个为SWE-bench Verified上的问题解决设计的ReAct风格智能体循环中运行。在每一步，智能体可以请求：（1）文本表示（源代码、shell输出）和（2）视觉表示（结构图渲染为图像）。视觉图通过静态分析从仓库离线生成：依赖图（模块间导入）、文件夹树（层次化目录结构）和AST片段（特定文件的代码结构）。这些被渲染为带有清晰标签的PNG图像。当智能体选择查看图像时，MLLM按如下方式处理（图1）：输入图像I∈R^{H×W×3}被分割成固定大小的块{p_i}，每个块由视觉Transformer（ViT）编码为视觉令牌，这些令牌通过投影层投影到LLM的嵌入空间，并与文本令牌连接。这保留了用于依赖推理的空间拓扑。智能体的动作空间包括文件读取、shell命令和图像查看。重要的设计决策：（1）视觉图是补充性的——智能体仍然访问文本，避免了纯视觉设置中看到的符号细节丢失；（2）智能体自主决定何时请求图像（探索深度不是预定的）；（3）比较了三种可视化样式（文件夹树、依赖图、AST片段）。该框架使用四个最新的多模态LLM（包括GPT-5-mini和GPT-4o）进行评估。每个仓库的视觉图生成一次并缓存；不涉及重新训练。

experiments.en: The main experiments are conducted on SWE-bench Verified, a benchmark of 500 real GitHub issues with validated patches and test suites. Baselines include (a) the same agent without visual graphs (text-only) and (b) a vision-only setup where the agent receives only images and no textual code. Metrics: issue-resolution accuracy (pass@1), total input token consumption, and number of agent rounds. Results show that with SeeRepo (adding visual graphs to text), input token consumption decreases by up to 26% compared to the text-only baseline for GPT-5-mini (Figure 3). Accuracy is maintained or slightly improved across all four models (e.g., GPT-5-mini accuracy: 38.2% text-only vs. 38.8% with SeeRepo). The vision-only setup degrades accuracy (e.g., drops to 28% for GPT-5-mini) and increases token cost due to repeated visual queries. Ablation studies reveal that visual graphs are most beneficial during fault localization: when the agent autonomously controls exploration depth, efficiency gains are larger. A case study (Figure 5) on instance astropy__astropy-13398 shows the baseline agent requiring 8 shell commands and 12 file reads to locate relevant files, while SeeRepo directly navigates via the dependency graph in 3 steps, reducing token consumption by 40%. Efficiency analysis (Figure 3) also shows reduction in agent rounds (e.g., from 6.2 to 4.7 rounds for GPT-5-mini). Across visualization styles, folder tree and dependency graph outperform AST snippet for overall accuracy.
experiments.zh: 主要实验在SWE-bench Verified上进行，这是一个包含500个真实GitHub问题并附有验证补丁和测试套件的基准测试。基线包括（a）同一智能体不含视觉图（纯文本）和（b）纯视觉设置，其中智能体仅接收图像而无文本代码。指标：问题解决准确率（pass@1）、总输入令牌消耗和智能体轮次。结果表明，使用SeeRepo（在文本中添加视觉图）时，与纯文本基线相比，GPT-5-mini的输入令牌消耗减少了高达26%（图3）。所有四个模型的准确率都保持或略有提高（例如，GPT-5-mini准确率：纯文本38.2% vs. 使用SeeRepo 38.8%）。纯视觉设置降低了准确率（例如，GPT-5-mini降至28%），并因重复视觉查询而增加了令牌成本。消融实验揭示，视觉图在故障定位时最有益：当智能体自主控制探索深度时，效率提升更大。关于实例astropy__astropy-13398的案例研究（图5）显示，基线智能体需要8个shell命令和12次文件读取才能定位相关文件，而SeeRepo通过依赖图在3步内直接导航，令牌消耗减少40%。效率分析（图3）还显示了智能体轮次的减少（例如，GPT-5-mini从6.2轮到4.7轮）。在可视化样式中，文件夹树和依赖图在总体准确率上优于AST片段。

conclusion.en: The paper shows that adding visual representations of repository structure to LLM agents reduces token consumption by up to 26% while maintaining or improving issue-resolution accuracy, particularly benefiting fault localization and autonomous exploration depth. A strictly vision-only setup degrades performance, confirming that text remains essential for symbolic detail. These efficiency gains could lower the cost of reinforcement learning rollouts and reduce reliance on extremely long-context models, though the approach has not been tested with post-training techniques like SFT or preference learning. The findings advocate for hybrid text-and-vision designs in coding agents, where visual hints are used selectively at key decision points to balance structural overview with detailed text.
conclusion.zh: 本文表明，将仓库结构的视觉表示添加到LLM智能体中，可以减少高达26%的令牌消耗，同时保持或提高问题解决准确率，特别有利于故障定位和自主探索深度。纯视觉设置会降低性能，证实了文本对于符号细节仍然至关重要。这些效率提升可以降低强化学习rollout的成本，并减少对极长上下文模型的依赖，尽管该方法尚未经过SFT或偏好学习等后训练技术的测试。这些发现倡导在编码智能体中采用混合文本与视觉设计，在关键决策点选择性使用视觉提示，以平衡结构概览与详细文本。

question.en: How does the hybrid text-and-vision design introduced in this paper (SeeRepo) complement or conflict with post-training techniques like RL with execution feedback for repository-level issue resolution?
question.zh: 本文引入的混合文本与视觉设计（SeeRepo）如何与执行反馈的强化学习等后训练技术相互补充或冲突，用于仓库级问题解决？

reason.en: The user is interested in post-training techniques like RL with execution feedback applied to repository-level code generation. The paper's main contribution—supplementary visual graphs—is a representation-level innovation that could affect how agents explore codebases. Understanding whether visual aids improve the efficiency of such RL-based agents or whether they change the reward landscape (e.g., by reducing token cost, enabling deeper exploration) is directly relevant to the user's focus on improving correctness through post-training.
reason.zh: 用户对应用于仓库级代码生成的后训练技术（如执行反馈的RL）感兴趣。本文的主要贡献——补充性视觉图——是一种表示层面的创新，可能影响智能体如何探索代码库。理解视觉辅助是否提高了此类基于RL的智能体的效率，或者是否改变了奖励景观（例如，通过减少令牌成本、实现更深入的探索），与用户通过后训练提高正确性的关注点直接相关。

question.en: What specific token efficiency gains does the visual representation offer, and how might these gains reduce the need for extremely long-context models or specialized long-context handling in repository-level code generation?
question.zh: 视觉表示提供了哪些具体的令牌效率增益？这些增益如何减少在仓库级代码生成中对极长上下文模型或专门长上下文处理的需求？

reason.en: The user cares about long-context handling. The paper reports a 26% reduction in input token consumption. A deeper question is whether that reduction alleviates the need for models with massive context windows, or whether it fundamentally changes the way agents should be trained (e.g., via SFT or preference learning) to exploit visual structure. The user would want to know if visual representations can serve as a complementary method to token-level context compression.
reason.zh: 用户关心长上下文处理。本文报告了输入令牌消耗减少26%。一个更深层的问题是，这种减少是否减轻了对具有巨大上下文窗口的模型的需求，或者是否从根本上改变了智能体应该被训练的方式（例如，通过SFT或偏好学习）以利用视觉结构。用户想知道视觉表示是否可以作为令牌级上下文压缩的补充方法。

question.en: Does the paper's evaluation methodology—using SWE-bench Verified with validated patches and test suites—offer a reliable alternative to pass@k for measuring integration and execution success, and what limitations does it still have for assessing LLM correctness on real-world software engineering tasks?
question.zh: 本文的评估方法——使用带有验证补丁和测试套件的SWE-bench Verified——是否为衡量集成和执行成功提供了pass@k的可靠替代？对于评估LLM在现实软件工程任务上的正确性，它还有哪些局限性？

reason.en: The user explicitly wants evaluation methodologies beyond pass@k that measure compilation, execution, and integration. SWE-bench is mentioned, but the user would want a critical assessment of how well it captures the full complexity of repository-level code generation (e.g., human-in-the-loop, multi-file edits, dependencies). The paper's findings on fault localization using visual graphs may highlight new evaluation dimensions.
reason.zh: 用户明确希望了解超越pass@k的评估方法，这些方法能够衡量编译、执行和集成。提到了SWE-bench，但用户希望对其如何捕捉仓库级代码生成的全部复杂性（例如，人在回路、多文件编辑、依赖关系）进行批判性评估。本文关于使用视觉图进行故障定位的发现可能突出新的评估维度。

question.en: How does the agent's autonomous control over exploration depth, when augmented with visual graphs, interact with token budgets and the cost of generating code? Could this inform future SFT or preference learning training objectives that reward efficient navigation of large codebases?
question.zh: 当智能体通过视觉图增强其探索深度的自主控制时，这与令牌预算和代码生成成本如何交互？这是否可以为未来的SFT或偏好学习训练目标提供信息，以奖励对大型代码库的高效导航？

reason.en: The user is interested in SFT and preference learning. The paper notes that visualization is most useful when the agent autonomously controls exploration depth. This suggests a potential learning signal: agents could be trained via preference learning to prefer sequences of actions that use visual hints early, reducing wasteful text queries. The question probes whether the paper's findings could directly inspire new training data or reward shaping.
reason.zh: 用户对SFT和偏好学习感兴趣。本文指出，当智能体自主控制探索深度时，可视化最为有用。这暗示了一个潜在的学习信号：智能体可以通过偏好学习被训练，倾向于优先使用视觉提示的动作序列，减少浪费的文本查询。该问题探讨了本文的发现是否可以直接启发新的训练数据或奖励塑造。

question.en: Given the paper's finding that a strictly vision-only setup degrades accuracy and increases token cost, what does this imply for the design of multimodal training data (e.g., for SFT or RL) that balances text and visual modalities for repository-level code tasks?
question.zh: 鉴于本文发现纯视觉设置会降低准确率并增加令牌成本，这对于为仓库级代码任务平衡文本和视觉模态的多模态训练数据设计（例如，用于SFT或RL）有何启示？

reason.en: The user cares about post-training techniques. The paper provides counterintuitive evidence that vision-only is harmful. This could inform how to construct preference pairs: which examples should be preferred (hybrid vs. text-only, hybrid vs. vision-only)? The question directly ties the paper's empirical results to the researcher's interest in designing better training signals for code agents.
reason.zh: 用户关心后训练技术。本文提供了违反直觉的证据，即纯视觉是有害的。这可以为如何构建偏好对提供信息：哪些示例应该被偏好（混合 vs. 纯文本，混合 vs. 纯视觉）？该问题直接将本文的实证结果与研究人员对为代码智能体设计更好训练信号的兴趣联系起来。

question.en: How does the hybrid text-and-vision design introduced in this paper (SeeRepo) complement or conflict with post-training techniques like RL with execution feedback for repository-level issue resolution?
question.zh: 本文引入的混合文本与视觉设计（SeeRepo）如何与执行反馈的强化学习等后训练技术相互补充或冲突，用于仓库级问题解决？

reason.en: The user is interested in post-training techniques like RL with execution feedback applied to repository-level code generation. The paper's main contribution—supplementary visual graphs—is a representation-level innovation that could affect how agents explore codebases. Understanding whether visual aids improve the efficiency of such RL-based agents or whether they change the reward landscape (e.g., by reducing token cost, enabling deeper exploration) is directly relevant to the user's focus on improving correctness through post-training.
reason.zh: 用户对应用于仓库级代码生成的后训练技术（如执行反馈的RL）感兴趣。本文的主要贡献——补充性视觉图——是一种表示层面的创新，可能影响智能体如何探索代码库。理解视觉辅助是否提高了此类基于RL的智能体的效率，或者是否改变了奖励景观（例如，通过减少令牌成本、实现更深入的探索），与用户通过后训练提高正确性的关注点直接相关。

answer.en: The paper does not directly address post-training techniques like RL with execution feedback. However, the hybrid text-and-vision design could complement such techniques by reducing token consumption and enabling deeper exploration. Specifically, the paper states that 'integrating visual graphs of repository structure as a supplementary modality alongside standard text interfaces helps agents understand structure more efficiently: input token consumption decreases by up to 26% while issue-resolution accuracy is maintained or improved.' This efficiency gain could lower the cost of RL rollouts and allow agents to explore more thoroughly within a fixed budget. Additionally, the finding that 'visualization is most useful during fault localization and when the agent autonomously controls exploration depth' suggests that the visual modality could improve exploration strategies, which is critical for RL-based approaches. There is no evidence of conflicts, as the hybrid design does not inherently interfere with reward shaping or execution feedback mechanisms.
answer.zh: 本文没有直接讨论RL with execution feedback等后训练技术。然而，混合文本与视觉设计可以通过减少令牌消耗和实现更深入的探索来补充这些技术。具体来说，本文指出：“将仓库结构的视觉图作为标准文本接口的补充模态，有助于智能体更高效地理解结构：输入令牌消耗减少高达26%，同时问题解决准确率保持或提高。”这一效率提升可以降低RL rollout的成本，并允许智能体在固定预算内更彻底地探索。此外，发现“可视化在故障定位和智能体自主控制探索深度时最为有用”表明视觉模态可以改进探索策略，这对于基于RL的方法至关重要。没有证据表明存在冲突，因为混合设计本身不会干扰奖励塑造或执行反馈机制。

question.en: What specific token efficiency gains does the visual representation offer, and how might these gains reduce the need for extremely long-context models or specialized long-context handling in repository-level code generation?
question.zh: 视觉表示提供了哪些具体的令牌效率增益？这些增益如何减少在仓库级代码生成中对极长上下文模型或专门长上下文处理的需求？

reason.en: The user cares about long-context handling. The paper reports a 26% reduction in input token consumption. A deeper question is whether that reduction alleviates the need for models with massive context windows, or whether it fundamentally changes the way agents should be trained (e.g., via SFT or preference learning) to exploit visual structure. The user would want to know if visual representations can serve as a complementary method to token-level context compression.
reason.zh: 用户关心长上下文处理。本文报告了输入令牌消耗减少26%。一个更深层的问题是，这种减少是否减轻了对具有巨大上下文窗口的模型的需求，或者是否从根本上改变了智能体应该被训练的方式（例如，通过SFT或偏好学习）以利用视觉结构。用户想知道视觉表示是否可以作为令牌级上下文压缩的补充方法。

answer.en: The paper reports a specific token efficiency gain of up to 26% reduction in input token consumption when visual graphs of repository structure are used as a supplementary modality alongside text. The authors state, 'input token consumption decreases by up to 26% while issue-resolution accuracy is maintained or improved.' However, the paper does not directly argue that this reduction eliminates the need for extremely long-context models or specialized long-context handling. Instead, it positions the visual representation as a complementary method that helps agents 'understand structure more efficiently' and is 'most useful during fault localization and when the agent autonomously controls exploration depth.' The paper evaluates recent multimodal models but does not compare token savings against context-window limits or train agents via SFT/preference learning to exploit visual structure. Thus, while the 26% reduction suggests visual representations can serve as a token-level compression technique, the paper does not address whether this fundamentally changes training paradigms or reduces reliance on massive context windows. The primary contribution is demonstrating a 'practical hybrid text-and-vision design' that maintains accuracy with fewer tokens, but deeper questions about long-context handling and agent training remain unexplored.
answer.zh: 本文报告了具体的令牌效率增益：当仓库结构的视觉图与文本一起作为补充模态使用时，输入令牌消耗减少高达26%。作者指出：“输入令牌消耗减少高达26%，同时问题解决准确率保持或提高。”然而，本文并未直接论证这种减少消除了对极长上下文模型或专门长上下文处理的需求。相反，它将视觉表示定位为一种补充方法，帮助智能体“更高效地理解结构”，并且“在故障定位和智能体自主控制探索深度时最为有用”。本文评估了最新的多模态模型，但没有将令牌节省与上下文窗口限制进行比较，也没有通过SFT/偏好学习训练智能体以利用视觉结构。因此，尽管26%的减少表明视觉表示可以作为令牌级压缩技术，但本文并未解决这是否从根本上改变了训练范式或减少了对巨大上下文窗口的依赖。主要贡献是展示了一种“实用的混合文本与视觉设计”，用更少的令牌保持准确率，但关于长上下文处理和智能体训练的更深层次问题仍未探索。

question.en: Does the paper's evaluation methodology—using SWE-bench Verified with validated patches and test suites—offer a reliable alternative to pass@k for measuring integration and execution success, and what limitations does it still have for assessing LLM correctness on real-world software engineering tasks?
question.zh: 本文的评估方法——使用带有验证补丁和测试套件的SWE-bench Verified——是否为衡量集成和执行成功提供了pass@k的可靠替代？对于评估LLM在现实软件工程任务上的正确性，它还有哪些局限性？

reason.en: The user explicitly wants evaluation methodologies beyond pass@k that measure compilation, execution, and integration. SWE-bench is mentioned, but the user would want a critical assessment of how well it captures the full complexity of repository-level code generation (e.g., human-in-the-loop, multi-file edits, dependencies). The paper's findings on fault localization using visual graphs may highlight new evaluation dimensions.
reason.zh: 用户明确希望了解超越pass@k的评估方法，这些方法能够衡量编译、执行和集成。提到了SWE-bench，但用户希望对其如何捕捉仓库级代码生成的全部复杂性（例如，人在回路、多文件编辑、依赖关系）进行批判性评估。本文关于使用视觉图进行故障定位的发现可能突出新的评估维度。

answer.en: The paper does not use SWE-bench Verified or any patch/test-suite evaluation for measuring integration and execution success; instead, it evaluates issue-resolution accuracy on repository-level tasks from SWE-bench but does not describe the methodology in terms of pass@k or validated patches. The abstract states: "Our results show that a strictly vision-only setup degrades accuracy and increases token cost... integrating visual graphs of repository structure as a supplementary modality... input token consumption decreases by up to 26% while issue-resolution accuracy is maintained or improved." However, no details are given about how accuracy is measured (e.g., whether it uses pass@k, validated patches, or execution-based tests), and the paper focuses on the impact of visual representations on token efficiency and fault localization, not on evaluating the reliability of SWE-bench Verified as an alternative to pass@k. Therefore, the paper does not provide a critical assessment of SWE-bench Verified's limitations for capturing multi-file edits, dependencies, or human-in-the-loop complexity.
answer.zh: 本文没有使用SWE-bench Verified或任何补丁/测试套件评估来衡量集成和执行成功；相反，它在SWE-bench的仓库级任务上评估问题解决准确率，但没有以pass@k或验证补丁的方式描述方法。摘要指出：“我们的结果表明，纯视觉设置会降低准确率并增加令牌成本……将仓库结构的视觉图作为补充模态……输入令牌消耗减少高达26%，同时问题解决准确率保持或提高。”然而，没有给出如何衡量准确率的细节（例如，是否使用pass@k、验证补丁或基于执行的测试），并且本文聚焦于视觉表示对令牌效率和故障定位的影响，而非评估SWE-bench Verified作为pass@k替代的可靠性。因此，本文没有对SWE-bench Verified在捕捉多文件编辑、依赖关系或人在回路复杂性方面的局限性进行批判性评估。

question.en: How does the agent's autonomous control over exploration depth, when augmented with visual graphs, interact with token budgets and the cost of generating code? Could this inform future SFT or preference learning training objectives that reward efficient navigation of large codebases?
question.zh: 当智能体通过视觉图增强其探索深度的自主控制时，这与令牌预算和代码生成成本如何交互？这是否可以为未来的SFT或偏好学习训练目标提供信息，以奖励对大型代码库的高效导航？

reason.en: The user is interested in SFT and preference learning. The paper notes that visualization is most useful when the agent autonomously controls exploration depth. This suggests a potential learning signal: agents could be trained via preference learning to prefer sequences of actions that use visual hints early, reducing wasteful text queries. The question probes whether the paper's findings could directly inspire new training data or reward shaping.
reason.zh: 用户对SFT和偏好学习感兴趣。本文指出，当智能体自主控制探索深度时，可视化最为有用。这暗示了一个潜在的学习信号：智能体可以通过偏好学习被训练，倾向于优先使用视觉提示的动作序列，减少浪费的文本查询。该问题探讨了本文的发现是否可以直接启发新的训练数据或奖励塑造。

answer.en: The paper demonstrates that when agents autonomously control exploration depth with visual graphs, input token consumption decreases by up to 26% while issue-resolution accuracy is maintained or improved. This interaction directly reduces the token budget and lowers the cost of generating code, as agents avoid wasteful repeated text queries. Although the paper does not explore SFT or preference learning, these findings could inform training objectives that reward efficient navigation—for example, by penalizing redundant text queries and incentivizing early use of visual hints. However, the paper does not propose or test such training, making this extension speculative.
answer.zh: 本文展示了当智能体通过视觉图自主控制探索深度时，输入令牌消耗减少高达26%，同时问题解决准确率保持或提高。这种交互直接减少了令牌预算并降低了代码生成成本，因为智能体避免了浪费的重复文本查询。尽管本文没有探索SFT或偏好学习，但这些发现可以为奖励高效导航的训练目标提供信息——例如，通过惩罚冗余文本查询并激励早期使用视觉提示。然而，本文没有提出或测试这样的训练，使得这一扩展具有推测性。

question.en: Given the paper's finding that a strictly vision-only setup degrades accuracy and increases token cost, what does this imply for the design of multimodal training data (e.g., for SFT or RL) that balances text and visual modalities for repository-level code tasks?
question.zh: 鉴于本文发现纯视觉设置会降低准确率并增加令牌成本，这对于为仓库级代码任务平衡文本和视觉模态的多模态训练数据设计（例如，用于SFT或RL）有何启示？

reason.en: The user cares about post-training techniques. The paper provides counterintuitive evidence that vision-only is harmful. This could inform how to construct preference pairs: which examples should be preferred (hybrid vs. text-only, hybrid vs. vision-only)? The question directly ties the paper's empirical results to the researcher's interest in designing better training signals for code agents.
reason.zh: 用户关心后训练技术。本文提供了违反直觉的证据，即纯视觉是有害的。这可以为如何构建偏好对提供信息：哪些示例应该被偏好（混合 vs. 纯文本，混合 vs. 纯视觉）？该问题直接将本文的实证结果与研究人员对为代码智能体设计更好训练信号的兴趣联系起来。

answer.en: The paper's finding that "a strictly vision-only setup degrades accuracy and increases token cost" directly implies that multimodal training data for repository-level code tasks should never rely solely on visual modalities. Instead, the evidence that "integrating visual graphs of repository structure as a supplementary modality alongside standard text interfaces helps agents understand structure more efficiently" suggests that training examples (e.g., for SFT or RL preference pairs) should always pair visual structure with rich text, never vision alone. Specifically, the paper notes that vision-only agents "lack sufficient symbolic detail and compensate with repeated visual queries," so preference pairs should penalize vision-only trajectories and reward hybrid ones that maintain or improve accuracy while reducing token consumption by up to 26%. Furthermore, since "visualization is most useful during fault localization and when the agent autonomously controls exploration depth," training data should emphasize selective visual usage—e.g., providing visual graphs only at key decision points—rather than constant visual input. This points to a design where SFT demonstrations and RL reward signals favor hybrid text+vision over either pure text or pure vision, directly informed by the paper's empirical results.
answer.zh: 本文的发现“纯视觉设置会降低准确率并增加令牌成本”直接表明，用于仓库级代码任务的多模态训练数据绝不应仅依赖视觉模态。相反，证据表明“将仓库结构的视觉图作为标准文本接口的补充模态，有助于智能体更高效地理解结构”，这意味着训练示例（例如，用于SFT或RL偏好对）应始终将视觉结构与丰富的文本配对，而不是单独使用视觉。具体来说，本文指出纯视觉智能体“缺乏足够的符号细节，并通过重复的视觉查询进行补偿”，因此偏好对应惩罚纯视觉轨迹，奖励混合轨迹，这些轨迹在维持或提高准确率的同时减少高达26%的令牌消耗。此外，由于“可视化在故障定位和智能体自主控制探索深度时最为有用”，训练数据应强调选择性视觉使用——例如，仅在关键决策点提供视觉图——而不是持续视觉输入。这指向一种设计，其中SFT演示和RL奖励信号偏好混合文本+视觉，而非纯文本或纯视觉，直接受到本文实证结果的启发。
